verses.text: The afflicted one begs God to hear and not hide His face 'in the day when I call.' His trouble drives him to prayer. The suffering soul most dreads the silence of God.
The afflicted one begs God to hear and not hide His face 'in the day when I call.' His trouble drives him to prayer. The suffering soul most dreads the silence of God.
His days vanish like smoke, his bones burn, his heart withers like grass, and he is lonely as a desert bird. Grief has wasted him. There are sorrows that leave a soul desolate and sleepless.
His days vanish like smoke, his bones burn, his heart withers like grass, and he is lonely as a desert bird. Grief has wasted him. There are sorrows that leave a soul desolate and sleepless.

His days vanish like smoke, his bones burn, his heart withers like grass, and he is lonely as a desert bird. Grief has wasted him. There are sorrows that leave a soul desolate and sleepless.
Mocked by enemies all day, he eats ashes and mingles tears with his drink, his life a lengthening shadow under God's indignation. His misery feels like wrath. Yet even here he keeps speaking to God.

'But thou, O LORD, shalt endure for ever,' and will arise to have mercy on Zion in her set time. He lifts his eyes from self to God. The eternal, unchanging God is the hope of His afflicted people.
'But thou, O LORD, shalt endure for ever,' and will arise to have mercy on Zion in her set time. He lifts his eyes from self to God. The eternal, unchanging God is the hope of His afflicted people.
'But thou, O LORD, shalt endure for ever,' and will arise to have mercy on Zion in her set time. He lifts his eyes from self to God. The eternal, unchanging God is the hope of His afflicted people.
The nations shall fear the LORD when He builds up Zion, for 'he will regard the prayer of the destitute.' God answers the lowly. The cry of the helpless is never beneath His notice.

This shall be written for the generation to come, that the LORD heard the groaning of the prisoner and loosed the condemned. His mercies are recorded. What God does for one age He preserves to encourage the next.

Weakened in the way, he prays, 'take me not away in the midst of my days,' for God's years endure through all generations. He pleads his frailty against God's eternity. The brevity of our life casts us on the everlasting God.

Weakened in the way, he prays, 'take me not away in the midst of my days,' for God's years endure through all generations. He pleads his frailty against God's eternity. The brevity of our life casts us on the everlasting God.

Of old God laid the earth's foundations; they shall perish and be changed, 'but thou art the same, and thy years shall have no end.' Hebrews applies this to Christ. The Son who made all things outlasts a fading creation, and His servants' children are kept secure.
